reasoning: The FRED data fetch shows a recent spike in the latest expected 1-year inflation reading to 3.259, noticeably above the 12-month average (~2.7). The linear model predicts mean reversion (a projected 1-month value of 2.793), suggesting the recent high value will likely not be sustained. The tool's P(value > threshold) estimate of 0.27 gives a clear baseline probability that the inflation expectation will exceed this spiked threshold. Over longer forecast horizons, according to standard stationary assumptions about expected inflation converging to the Fed's 2% target (and moving probabilistically like a random walk around that mean), uncertainty increases. Hence, the probability curve gradually approaches 0.5 for the more distant dates, reflecting increased uncertainty over long-term structural changes in the economy.
